meta.card_contract: per-axis {value[0,1], confidence, cost, direction, strength, data_origin, finding, interpretation, source}

data_origin_legend.synthetic: not available in OT; placeholder prior (flag for VoI/run_experiment)

meta.note: Descriptive report-style cards resembling a Virtual-Biotech division dossier. Real where OT reaches; synthetic axes explicitly flagged. No matches faked — the arena produces matches from these cards.

narrative.target_overview: BRAF — B-Raf proto-oncogene, serine/threonine kinase. Protein kinase involved in the transduction of mitogenic signals from the cell membrane to the nucleus (Probable). Phosphorylates MAP2K1, and thereby activates the MAP kinase signal transduction pathway (PubMed:21441910,
pathways: Gain-of-function MRAS complexes activate RAF signaling
Signalling to p38 via RIT and RIN
Negative feedback regulation of MAPK pathway
SHOC2 M1731 mutant abolishes MRAS complex function
liabilities: 1 curated safety liability(ies) (heart disease) and 77 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal parietal bone morphology, decreased white adipose tissue amount, abnormal vascular endothelial cell morphology.
evidence_gaps: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (moderately specific): mutant-BRAF melanoma is the definitional driver context.

proposed_experiments.rationale: resolves the only unmeasured axis; likely the highest-VoI action

right_target.finding: Open Targets overall disease association with melanoma = 0.701 (aggregating genetic, somatic, literature and known-drug evidence). Loss-of-function constraint LOEUF (o/e)=0.15, in the constrained tail — the gene is intolerant to LoF, which raises both a mechanistic causality prior and an on-target-toxicity flag.
right_target.interpretation: Strong, multi-modal causal support: BRAF is a well-credentialled melanoma target and the association is unlikely to be literature-only noise. Sits on Gain-of-function MRAS complexes activate RAF signaling, Signalling to p38 via RIT and RIN.

right_tissue.finding: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (moderately specific): mutant-BRAF melanoma is the definitional driver context.
right_tissue.interpretation: UNMEASURED axis — this is the highest-value action for the VoI loop to resolve via a tier-3 run_experiment (single-cell profiling). The prior says BRAF is moderately specific; if it holds, tissue specificity is neutral — unlikely to decide the ranking alone. Until measured, treat this value as a hypothesis, not evidence.

right_safety.finding: 1 curated safety liability(ies) (heart disease) and 77 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal parietal bone morphology, decreased white adipose tissue amount, abnormal vascular endothelial cell morphology.
right_safety.interpretation: Safety is a headwind: the breadth of knockout phenotypes (77) and the curated liability signal point to a broad developmental/essential role, so systemic inhibition risks on-target toxicity in normal tissue — the therapeutic window is narrow and must be bought back with tumour-selective delivery or dosing.

right_patient.finding: Real clinical precedent: a drug against BRAF has reached PHASE_3 in melanoma (from Open Targets drugAndClinicalCandidates), so a treatable patient population is already defined. The specific biomarker stratum is a synthetic placeholder.
right_patient.interpretation: Patient axis is a strength: an existing PHASE_3 programme means the stratification and endpoint risk is largely retired — the open question is only which biomarker sub-population to enrich, not whether one exists.

right_commercial.finding: Competitive intensity is approximated from BRAF's prominence as a melanoma target (high-association targets tend to be crowded); the explicit competitor count and whitespace read are synthesised.
right_commercial.interpretation: Crowded field: BRAF is a well-trodden target, so commercial differentiation must come from a novel modality, payload, or indication niche rather than the target itself.

tractability.finding: Open Targets SM-modality tractability buckets satisfied: Approved Drug, Structure with Ligand, High-Quality Ligand, High-Quality Pocket, Druggable Family.
tractability.interpretation: De-risked modality: BRAF already has an approved small_molecule drug, so chemistry/biologics feasibility is proven and the programme starts from precedent rather than discovery risk.

narrative.target_overview: MAP2K1 — mitogen-activated protein kinase kinase 1. Dual specificity protein kinase which acts as an essential component of the MAP kinase signal transduction pathway. Binding of extracellular ligands such as growth factors, cytokines and hormones to their cell-surface re
pathways: Negative feedback regulation of MAPK pathway
Signaling by BRAF and RAF1 fusions
liabilities: 0 curated safety liability(ies) and 24 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal trophoblast layer morphology, increased cranium length, increased astrocyte number.

Signaling by BRAF and RAF1 fusions

narrative.target_overview: ATM — ATM serine/threonine kinase. Serine/threonine protein kinase which activates checkpoint signaling upon double strand breaks (DSBs), apoptosis and genotoxic stresses such as ionizing ultraviolet A light (UVA), thereby acting as a DNA damage sensor (P
pathways: Presynaptic phase of homologous DNA pairing and strand exchange
Regulation of TP53 Activity through Phosphorylation
Autodegradation of the E3 ubiquitin ligase COP1
liabilities: 10 curated safety liability(ies) (diabetes, vomiting, growth retardation) and 132 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal cerebellar molecular layer, decreased IgG level, female infertility.
evidence_gaps: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (broadly expressed / non-specific): housekeeping DNA-damage sensor; expressed everywhere.

proposed_experiments.rationale: resolves the only unmeasured axis; likely the highest-VoI action

right_target.finding: Open Targets overall disease association with melanoma = 0.617 (aggregating genetic, somatic, literature and known-drug evidence). Loss-of-function constraint LOEUF (o/e)=0.77, in the tolerant range — LoF is well tolerated in population data, weakly arguing against essential-gene toxicity.
right_target.interpretation: Strong, multi-modal causal support: ATM is a well-credentialled melanoma target and the association is unlikely to be literature-only noise. Sits on DNA Damage/Telomere Stress Induced Senescence, Presynaptic phase of homologous DNA pairing and strand exchange.

right_tissue.finding: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (broadly expressed / non-specific): housekeeping DNA-damage sensor; expressed everywhere.
right_tissue.interpretation: UNMEASURED axis — this is the highest-value action for the VoI loop to resolve via a tier-3 run_experiment (single-cell profiling). The prior says ATM is broadly expressed / non-specific; if it holds, tissue specificity is a liability that a tumour-selective modality would have to compensate for. Until measured, treat this value as a hypothesis, not evidence.

right_safety.finding: 10 curated safety liability(ies) (diabetes, vomiting, growth retardation) and 132 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal cerebellar molecular layer, decreased IgG level, female infertility.
right_safety.interpretation: Safety is a headwind: the breadth of knockout phenotypes (132) and the curated liability signal point to a broad developmental/essential role, so systemic inhibition risks on-target toxicity in normal tissue — the therapeutic window is narrow and must be bought back with tumour-selective delivery or dosing.

right_patient.finding: No melanoma clinical precedent for ATM in Open Targets, so both the patient population and its enrichment biomarker would have to be established de novo (synthetic).
right_patient.interpretation: Patient axis is unproven: with no clinical footprint, ATM carries first-in-class stratification risk, and the arena should discount confidence here accordingly.

right_commercial.finding: Competitive intensity is approximated from ATM's prominence as a melanoma target (high-association targets tend to be crowded); the explicit competitor count and whitespace read are synthesised.
right_commercial.interpretation: Crowded field: ATM is a well-trodden target, so commercial differentiation must come from a novel modality, payload, or indication niche rather than the target itself.

tractability.finding: Open Targets SM-modality tractability buckets satisfied: Structure with Ligand, High-Quality Ligand, Druggable Family.
tractability.interpretation: Tractable by small_molecule: a druggable pocket / structural handle is present, so the modality is credible though not yet clinically validated for this target.

narrative.target_overview: AKT1 — AKT serine/threonine kinase 1. AKT1 is one of 3 closely related serine/threonine-protein kinases (AKT1, AKT2 and AKT3) called the AKT kinase, and which regulate many processes including metabolism, proliferation, cell survival, growth and angiogenesis
pathways: PI5P, PP2A and IER3 Regulate PI3K/AKT Signaling
PTK6 Regulates RTKs and Their Effectors AKT1 and DOK1
liabilities: 4 curated safety liability(ies) (heart disease, apoptototic cell death in cancer cells, hyperglycemia) and 60 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal thymus morphology, pallor, abnormal maternal decidual layer morphology.
evidence_gaps: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (broadly expressed / non-specific): broadly expressed node; specificity relies entirely on context.

proposed_experiments.rationale: resolves the only unmeasured axis; likely the highest-VoI action

narrative.target_overview: ERBB2 — erb-b2 receptor tyrosine kinase 2. Protein tyrosine kinase that is part of several cell surface receptor complexes, but that apparently needs a coreceptor for ligand binding. Essential component of a neuregulin-receptor complex, although neuregulins do no
pathways: PI5P, PP2A and IER3 Regulate PI3K/AKT Signaling
Resistance of ERBB2 KD mutants to trastuzumab
Drug resistance in ERBB2 TMD/JMD mutants
liabilities: 3 curated safety liability(ies) (heart disease, cardiotoxicity, more sensitive to trastuzamab) and 52 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal phrenic nerve morphology, decreased Schwann cell precursor number, embryonic lethality, complete penetrance.
evidence_gaps: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (sharply tumour-restricted): HER2-high sub-population is sharply demarcated — a clean ADC window.

proposed_experiments.rationale: resolves the only unmeasured axis; likely the highest-VoI action

The prior says ERBB2 is sharply tumour-restricted; if it holds, tissue specificity is a decisive strength that can offset a weaker tractability or safety axis. Until measured, treat this value as a hypothesis, not evidence.

Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: abnormal phrenic nerve morphology, decreased Schwann cell precursor number, embryonic lethality, complete penetrance.
right_safety.interpretation: Safety is a headwind: the breadth of knockout phenotypes (52) and the curated liability signal point to a broad developmental/essential role, so systemic inhibition risks on-target toxicity in normal tissue — the therapeutic window is narrow and must be bought back with tumour-selective delivery or dosing.

right_patient.interpretation: Patient axis is unproven: with no clinical footprint, ERBB2 carries first-in-class stratification risk, and the arena should discount confidence here accordingly.

narrative.target_overview: SMARCA4 — SWI/SNF related BAF chromatin remodeling complex subunit ATPase 4. pathways: Formation of neuronal progenitor and neuronal BAF (npBAF and nBAF)

narrative.target_overview: NTRK1 — neurotrophic receptor tyrosine kinase 1. Receptor tyrosine kinase involved in the development and the maturation of the central and peripheral nervous systems through regulation of proliferation, differentiation and survival of sympathetic and nervous neurons.
pathways: Signalling to p38 via RIT and RIN
liabilities: 15 curated safety liability(ies) (grey matter heterotopia, weight gain, seizures) and 48 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: decreased body size, no abnormal phenotype detected, anhidrosis.
evidence_gaps: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. Value shown is a designed prior (sharply tumour-restricted): highly lineage-restricted expression (strong specificity prior); but antibody-only tractability caps the modality options.

proposed_experiments.rationale: resolves the only unmeasured axis; likely the highest-VoI action

right_target.finding: Open Targets overall disease association with melanoma = 0.546 (aggregating genetic, somatic, literature and known-drug evidence). right_target.interpretation: Strong, multi-modal causal support: NTRK1 is a well-credentialled melanoma target and the association is unlikely to be literature-only noise. Sits on Signalling to p38 via RIT and RIN, Signalling to RAS.

right_tissue.finding: Single-cell tissue specificity (tau) and malignant-vs-stromal fraction are NOT available from Open Targets. right_tissue.interpretation: UNMEASURED axis — this is the highest-value action for the VoI loop to resolve via a tier-3 run_experiment (single-cell profiling). The prior says NTRK1 is sharply tumour-restricted; if it holds, tissue specificity is a decisive strength that can offset a weaker tractability or safety axis. Until measured, treat this value as a hypothesis, not evidence.

right_safety.finding: 15 curated safety liability(ies) (grey matter heterotopia, weight gain, seizures) and 48 distinct mouse-knockout phenotypes on record. Representative mouse-KO phenotypes: decreased body size, no abnormal phenotype detected, anhidrosis.
right_safety.interpretation: Safety is a headwind: the breadth of knockout phenotypes (48) and the curated liability signal point to a broad developmental/essential role, so systemic inhibition risks on-target toxicity in normal tissue — the therapeutic window is narrow and must be bought back with tumour-selective delivery or dosing.
